macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Fourth Perek: Sanctified Items That Lack Me'ilah Status

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks on the progression of the masechet and reminder of me'ilah principles
Distinction between items that become pasul and items destined to die: both may retain me'ilah unless completely הולך לאיבוד

macro_segments.title: Chatat Animals That Must Die: Categories and the Principle of No Me'ilah

micro_segments.title: Three unconditional cases requiring death: velad chatat, tmurat chatat, and chatat of deceased owners
Two conditional cases: animals over one year old or lost-and-found with blemishes; distinction based on whether replacement already brought
Halacha that chatat destined to die cannot effect temurah and has no me'ilah: explanation via removal from kodshei Hashem category
Gemara: Why distinction between first three cases (unconditional death) and last two (conditional on replacement)?
Answer: In first three, death is inevitable regardless of circumstances; in last two, replacement determines necessity of death
micro_segments.display_title: Psika lei vs. lo psika l…

macro_segments.title: Money Set Aside for a Nazir's Korbonot: Unstipulated Funds and the Shelamim Default

micro_segments.title: Case of nazir who sanctified money without specifying allocation: could all be for shelamim, hence no me'ilah

macro_segments.title: Nazir's Funds After Death: Differential Status of Chatat, Olah, and Shelamim Money

micro_segments.title: Case of unstipulated ('sealed') money: defaults to olah; reasoning why chatat and shelamim are ruled out in death scenarios
Money set aside with explicit allocations: if nazir dies, chatat funds destroyed (no me'ilah), olah funds brought with me'ilah, shelamim funds brought with partial nazir status
Shelamim money brought after nazir's death: eaten one day only (not two), but no bread brought (unlike normal shelamim of living nazir)

macro_segments.title: Money Set Aside for a Nazir's Korbonot: Unstipulated Funds and the Shelamim Default (continued)

micro_segments.title: Gemara question: Why doesn't the same apply to bird offerings (kinim) which also lack clear allocation?
Answer via Rava: Torah's expectation is purchase of immediately usable korban; birds too young would violate this principle

macro_segments.title: Birds (Kinim) Unsuitable by Age: Why No Me'ilah Even for Wrong-Aged Offerings

micro_segments.title: Background: Kinim (birds) unlike other offerings have no pidyon; קדושה cannot transfer even with blemish; too-young or too-old birds are הולך לאיבוד
No me'ilah by birds unsuitable by age: parallel to chatat shemetah (destined to die), completely removed from kodshei Hashem category

macro_segments.title: Blood of Sacrifices: Paradoxical Absence of Me'ilah Before Sprinking, Paradoxical Presence After

micro_segments.title: Mishnah teaches: before zerikat hadam, no me'ilah by blood; after blood goes to Kidron Valley, there is me'ilah

macro_segments.title: Wine Libations (Nesachim): Standard Me'ilah Before Pouring, Loss After

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: wine for libations has me'ilah initially but not after being poured into funnels and altar cavity
Contrast with blood: wine follows standard na'asah mitzvaso rule (no me'ilah once mitzvah complete), blood does not

macro_segments.title: Blood of Sacrifices: Paradoxical Absence of Me'ilah Before Sprinking, Paradoxical Presence After (continued)

micro_segments.title: Tannaitic dispute: Rabbi Meir says me'ilah applies to blood; Chakhamim say no me'ilah; source and reasoning
Ullas explanation: pasuk 'va'ani netativ lachem al hamizbe'ach lekapper' — blood serves atonement function, not fully kodshei Hashem
Debeі Rabbi Yishmael: 'lichapparah netativ velo leme'ilah' — exemption from me'ilah via atonement purpose
Rabbi Yochanan: gezeirah shava from 'ki hadam hu banefesh' — blood has single status before and after zerikat hadam, hence no me'ilah either way
Gemara challenge: Why assume no me'ilah before if it's נעשה מצותו status change after? Answer via principle that once mitzvah done, no me'ilah can apply

macro_segments.title: Exception to Na'asah Mitzvaso Rule: Terumат Hadeshen and Other Boundary Cases

micro_segments.title: Gemara cites terumas hadeshen and se'ir hamishtalach as examples where me'ilah continues despite mitzvah completion
Terumas hadeshen retains me'ilah because of 'vesamo sham' — placement requirement implies continuous status preservation
Principle: two written sources coming together do not generate a rule; exceptions remain limited to specified cases

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar bar Tzadok on Wine Clots: The Lul Tunnel, Cleaning Ritual, and Burning in Sanctity

micro_segments.title: Description: small tunnel (lul) beneath altar where wine and blood clots accumulate; cleaned once every seventy years by child priests
Congealed wine extracted and burned in sanctity (bikdusha) like defective consecrated items; source from pasuk 'bakodesh hasach nesech'
Gemara debate: whether Rabbi Elazar's requirement to burn wine implies doritta or derabbanan me'ilah status; resolution that derabbanan burning is sufficient

topical_tags.term: Lo nehenin v'lo mo'alin (not benefited from, no me'ilah)
Torim and benei yonah (doves and young pigeons)
Shnei ktuvim habaim k'echad (two written sources as one)
